label: Platform restructure: Build, Substrate, Tools, Settings
description: Cleans up the platform tier of Rippling's IA. (1) Banking folds into Finance — one place for spend. (2) Custom Apps becomes Studio — the build-artifacts suite (custom apps, reports, dashboards, workflow studio, developer, recipes). (3) Data becomes the raw-substrate suite (data catalog, pipelines, transformations, external objects). (4) Tools holds cross-cutting utilities (inbox, approvals, documents, audit log, meetings, partner admin). (5) Settings is dedicated to company configuration. (6) PEO suite drops — its compliance artifacts (workplace posters, state tax accounts) move into HR; PEO and HR Services become plans, not nav entries. (7) Time absorbs all time-off concepts (PTO and Leaves move out of HR).

ops.label: External Objects
ops.source: rippling-webapp/app/products/platform/HubPlatform/modules/ExternalObjects (Data Bridge & Connectors team per Data Cloud QPR — BigQuery, Databricks, Snowflake federation)

ops.label: Dashboards
ops.source: Data Cloud Q2 QPR — Analytics sub-team owns Reports + Dashboards as parallel surfaces (own design lead, eng manager, p95 < 5s goal)

ops.label: Rippling PEO
ops.description: Co-employment client. Rippling is the legal employer of record; HR/Payroll/Benefits/Time/Compliance are bundled and delivered through Rippling's PEO entity.

ops.label: HR Services
ops.description: ASO client. Rippling provides HR admin services (payroll tax, workers comp, compliance support) but is not the legal employer of record. PEO-lite.

_notes: manual-user-management (separate spoke MANUAL_USER_MANAGEMENT) is folded into the unified user-management app.
Directory is new — the catalog has no Directory app today.

PEO and HR Services (ASO) both become *plans*, not suites/apps — they're commercial modes (clientType), not separate product surfaces. The HR-resident compliance apps (Workplace Posters, State Tax Accounts) gate on `isPeoClient || isAsoClient` to render for either co-employment mode.

HR Services was modeled as an app under HR with no L3 nav and no real /hr-services route — it was always a clientType gate, not a destination. Promoted to plan to match how it actually works in the live product.
PEO Overview is dropped from L2 nav — would become an HR-home dashboard card for PEO customers (out of scope for L2 catalog).
PEO Join / PEO Leave flows from the backend (is_peo_join_visible, is_peo_leave_visible) are not modeled here; cross-sell to non-PEO customers can be handled at the plan level later.

Forecasting removed: the standalone HR Forecasting tile is being superseded by Workforce Management labor-plan surfaces per the 2026-Q2 Workforce Management QPR. The backend (app/forecasting/) continues as plumbing for those new surfaces, but the L2 nav tile no longer warrants its own real estate.
Global Payroll merged into Payroll: live product is moving toward a single unified payroll surface. Drop the separate Global Payroll L2 in favor of one Payroll entry that handles US + international.

Global suite removed: 'Global Workforce' was modeling a cross-sell tile as a fake suite. Real product (Global Payroll) lives in the Payroll suite. Cross-sell tiles for unowned bundles are a separate modeling concern documented in docs/cross-sell.md.

Data + Studio split: 'Data' owns the substrate (Pipelines, Transformations, Custom Objects, Custom Fields, Data Manager), 'Studio' owns build artifacts (Custom Apps, Reports, Workflows, Recipes, Forms, Tasks). The line between report/dashboard/custom app is fuzzy by design — Studio collects all admin-built artifacts under one suite. Custom Fields promoted from L3 (Settings > Company Details) to L2 in Data.

Inbox consolidation: per the 2026-Q2 Tasks & Approvals QPR, Approvals + Tasks are merging into a single unified Inbox. Notifications follows the same pattern (passive updates ≈ inbox items). v1 drops Approvals and Notification Center as separate L2 entries; Inbox is the single destination.

Data Manager → Data Catalog: live product is migrating /data-manager → /data/catalog (gated by data-app FF) per Data Cloud Q2 QPR. Same surface, expanded scope (semantic layer, search, lineage, Data Answers). v1 ships the new branding.
